claim: The mine failed to comply with REG-01, as the methane monitoring system did not automatically cut off power to electrical equipment when the 1.0% CH4 threshold was reached, contributing to the accident
warrant: The evidence shows that methane accumulation was a primary cause of the accident, and REG-01 requires automatic power cutoff to prevent such accidents. Compliance with REG-01 would have prevented or mitigated the accident

claim: The mine's ventilation scheme did not meet the requirements of REG-02, as it failed to prevent methane accumulation in sub-conveyor zones, contributing to the accident
warrant: The evidence shows that the ventilation scheme was inadequate, and REG-02 requires that ventilation schemes be designed to prevent methane accumulation. Compliance with REG-02 would have prevented or mitigated the accident

claim: The mine failed to comply with REG-03, as it did not conduct pre-drainage boreholes for the companion seam, contributing to methane accumulation and the accident
warrant: The evidence shows that methane accumulation from the companion seam was a primary cause of the accident, and REG-03 requires pre-drainage boreholes to prevent such accumulation. Compliance with REG-03 would have prevented or mitigated the accident

claim: The mine used non-explosion-proof equipment, violating REG-05, which contributed to the ignition of the methane-air mixture
warrant: The evidence shows that mechanical sparks from equipment were a probable ignition source, and REG-05 requires that all electrical equipment be explosion-proof. Compliance with REG-05 would have prevented or mitigated the accident

claim: The mine failed to comply with REG-06, as it did not maintain adequate stone dusting and barrier requirements, contributing to the coal dust explosion
warrant: The evidence shows that coal dust was a primary fuel for the explosion, and REG-06 requires adequate dust control measures. Compliance with REG-06 would have prevented or mitigated the accident
